question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set both `X-Real-IP` and `X-Forwarded-For` headers simultaneously, knowing that `X-Real-IP` takes priority, to suppress the real forwarded-for chain from being logged, hiding the true network path and making it impossible to diagnose a network partition caused by a compromised intermediate proxy?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths containing Unicode right-to-left override characters (e.g., `\\u202e`) so that the log message appears to show a different path when rendered in a log viewer, causing operators to misidentify which endpoint is being attacked during a network partition event?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests that all complete with 5xx status codes, causing `log.Info` to emit a flood of error-status log lines that overwhelm a log aggregator's ingest rate limit, causing log loss and blinding operators to the onset of a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Forwarded-For` to `0.0.0.0` to make their requests appear to come from an unroutable address, causing IP-based anomaly detection systems to ignore the traffic while the attacker floods the node with requests that exhaust resources and trigger a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with extremely long HTTP method strings (e.g., 1 MB `AAAA...`) so that `fmt.Sprintf` allocates a large string for each log message, and under concurrent load, this exhausts heap memory and crashes the process, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: internalPaths] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to `/health/liveness#fragment` or `/health/liveness/` (with trailing slash) so that `request.URL.RequestURI()` returns a string not present in `internalPaths`, causing health-check traffic to be logged at `log.Info` level and, at high volume, saturating the log pipeline and masking a real partition event?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that contains a null byte (`/network/list%00`) so that downstream log processors that treat null as a string terminator truncate the log entry, causing incomplete log records that prevent accurate reconstruction of network partition timelines?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to a multiline value (e.g., `1.2.3.4\\r\\nX-Injected: evil`) to perform HTTP header injection through the log output, causing log-shipping agents that parse raw log lines to misinterpret the injected header as a separate log field, corrupting partition-detection telemetry?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` body that never terminates, holding the `inner.ServeHTTP` call open indefinitely, accumulating open goroutines until the Go runtime runs out of stack space and crashes, partitioning the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.Write()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response path where `Write()` is called with a nil or zero-length slice, causing `w.data` to be set to an empty byte slice, and if a health-check response is affected, the health controller returns an empty body that causes the orchestrator to mark the node as unhealthy and remove it from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Forwarded-For` to a value containing a space followed by a valid IP (e.g., ` 1.2.3.4`) so that the entire string including the leading space is logged, causing IP-based log parsers to fail to extract the client IP and breaking network partition correlation rules?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path of `/health/readiness?slow=1` to a slow backend, causing the readiness check to time out and return 503, and if the attacker does this repeatedly, causing the orchestrator to continuously restart the node, effectively partitioning it from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a very large number of query parameters in the path (e.g., `?a=1&b=2&...` with 100,000 parameters) so that `request.URL.RequestURI()` returns a multi-megabyte string that is embedded in every log message, exhausting disk space and causing the process to hang on log writes, partitioning the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to a value that mimics a known Hedera consensus node IP address, causing the Rosetta node's logs to show that consensus node as the source of malformed requests, triggering automated firewall rules that block the real consensus node and partition the Rosetta node from the Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with HTTP/1.0 `Connection: close` headers at high frequency, causing the server to open and close TCP connections rapidly, exhausting ephemeral port space and preventing the node from establishing new outbound connections to Hedera consensus nodes, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that is a very long string of `/` characters (e.g., 1 MB of slashes) so that `request.URL.RequestURI()` returns a huge string, and `fmt.Sprintf` allocates a large buffer for each log message, causing memory exhaustion under concurrent load and crashing the process, partitioning the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Forwarded-For` to a value containing a valid IP followed by a comma and a private IP (e.g., `8.8.8.8, 192.168.1.1`), causing the entire string to be logged as the client IP, and if log-based firewall rules only check for exact IP matches, the attacker's real IP is never blocked, allowing sustained flooding that partitions the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `Host` header containing a very long string, and since `request.URL.RequestURI()` may include host information in some configurations, cause oversized log messages that exhaust disk space and partition the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.WriteHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response where `WriteHeader()` is never called (e.g., by causing a panic in the inner handler that is recovered), causing `w.statusCode` to remain at the default `http.StatusOK` (200), so that error responses are logged as successful, hiding network partition-related errors from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to an empty string (explicitly set to `\

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path containing format string directives (e.g., `/network/%s/%d`) so that if the path is ever passed to a format function elsewhere in the logging pipeline, it causes a format string vulnerability that crashes the log processor and blinds operators to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a very large `statusCode` value by triggering a non-standard HTTP response code from the inner handler, causing the log message to contain an unexpected integer that breaks log parsers relying on fixed-width status code fields, corrupting partition-detection dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Forwarded-For` to a value containing a valid IP and then a semicolon followed by a shell command (e.g., `1.2.3.4; rm -rf /logs`), and if the log output is ever processed by a shell script in the log pipeline, cause command injection that destroys log files and blinds operators to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that contains a percent-encoded newline (`%0a`) so that `request.URL.RequestURI()` returns a string with a literal newline, splitting the log entry into two lines and injecting a fake log entry that mimics a network partition alert, causing a false-positive automated response?
